title: Our times are in Thy hand
firstLine: Our times are in Thy hand;
publicDomainBasis: William Freeman Lloyd (1791–1853), Henri Abraham Cesar Malan (1787–1864). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
Verse 2
sections.text:
Our times are in Thy hand:
Whatever they may be;
Pleasing or painful, dark or bright,
As best may seem to Thee.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Our times are in Thy hand;
Why should we doubt or fear?
A father’s hand will never cause
His child a needless tear.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
Our times are in Thy hand;
Jesus, the Crucified,
Whose hand our many sins have pierced,
Is now our guard and guide.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
Our times are in Thy hand;
We’ll always trust to Thee,
Till we possess the promised crown,
And all Thy glory see.
